hypothesis: An MCP tool surface with claim/lease will prevent duplicate agent work without needing Git-style merges.
action_taken: Implemented claim/lease semantics in the tool contract and prototype server stub.
result: Able to reject non-claimant updates deterministically; append-only logs remained merge-safe.
conclusion: Claim/lease + optimistic concurrency is sufficient for POC coordination.
next_step: Implement idempotency by record ID for append-only tools.

hypothesis: Simple prefix matching will catch most accidental secret pastes without too many false positives.
action_taken: Drafted rules for common prefixes (AKIA, ghp_, xoxb-, PEM markers).
result: Catches obvious secrets; unclear on false positives for high-entropy random IDs.
conclusion: Balanced approach needed: prefix patterns + allowlist fields + optional entropy check.
next_step: Decide false-positive tolerance and add a documented override workflow.
